title: Veteran/Military Hotlines - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Veteran/Military Hotlines 4 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF LINES FOR LIFE MILITARY HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a helpline giving information about benefits, PTSD, substance use disorders, anger management issues, and counseling in regard to suicidal thoughts. 5100 SW Macadam Avenue Suite 400 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Individuals who are active duty military, veterans, and their families Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (888) 457-4838 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MILITARY ONESOURCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides phone and internet support service through the Department of Defense, offering assistance and resources to military personnel and their families on various issues. Services include counseling and information on personal issues, money matters, career issues, parenting, legal issues, elder care, relocation, violence, trauma, and transition to civilian life. Website Eligibility: Individuals who meet one or more of the following criteria: -Active duty service member of the Army, Marine Corps, Navy, Air Force, or Space Force and their immediate family members. This includes spouses, children, and anyone who has legal resposibility for the active duty service member's children. -Service members of the National Guard and reserve and their immediate family members. This includes spouses, children, and anyone who has legal resposibility for the active duty service member's children. -Coast Guard members who are activated as part of the Department of the Navy under Title 10 authority, as well as their immediate family members -Survivors who have not remarried and children of active-duty, National Guard, and reserve service members (regardless of activation status or cause of service member's death) -Medically discharged service members and their immediate family members currently enrolled in their service branch's wounded warrior or seriously ill and injured program -Retired service members and their immediate family members until 365 days past end of tour of service, retirement date or discharge date, including service members on the Temporary Disability Retirement List. Coast Guard veterans and their immediate family are eligible from their separation date until 365 days past end of tour of service -Discharged service members (if discharged honorably) and their immediate family members until 365 days past end of tour of service, retirement date or discharge date. Coast Guard veterans (if discharged honorably) and their immediate family are eligible from their separation date until 365 days past end of tour of service. -Designated Department of Defense (DOD) expeditionary civilians, as defined by Directive-type Memorandum (DTM)-17-004, “Department of Defense Expeditionary Civilian Workforce” during the 90 days prior to deployment and 180 days post-deployment. -Immediate family of designated DOD expeditionary civilians, including spouses, children and anyone who has legal responsibility for workforce member’s children, for the benefit of the children -Military academy cadets, students enrolled in one of the service academies for the Army, Navy or Air Force -Active-duty foreign military service members assigned to U.S. Army, Marine Corps, Navy, Air Force and Space Force installations who are registered in DEERS. Foreign military service members who have DOD-issued CAC with MWR/commissary/exchange privileges. -Immediate family members of foreign military active-duty service members registered in Defense Enrollment Eligibility Reporting System (DEERS). This includes spouse as defined by 10 USC Section 101 and children. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (800) 342-9647 Visit Website More Details VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA SUICIDE PREVENTION TEAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides access to resources for individuals in crisis situations. 308 SW 1st Avenue Suite 155 Portland, OR 972043432 Eligibility: Individuals who are veterans and their friends and families Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week 988 1 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VETS4WARRIORS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential, personalized, peer support. Helps people navigate the military and VA systems, connects callers with community support, and provides callers with the opportunity to talk about their challenges before they are in crisis. Eligibility: Active Duty, National Guard Service Members, Reservists, veterans, their families and caregivers, and anyone who knows of a veteran or service member who is struggling Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (855) 838-8255 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINES FOR LIFE MILITARY HELPLINE VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA SUICIDE PREVENTION TEAM
